panels.title: Total documents
panels.description: Total library-visible documents currently in the corpus.

panels.title: Total pages
panels.description: Total pages across library-visible documents.

panels.title: Verification
panels.description: Share of library-visible documents whose citation metadata is verified.

panels.title: DOI coverage
panels.description: Share of library-visible documents with either a recorded DOI or an explicit confirmed No DOI decision.

panels.title: App cert days
panels.description: Days until the public main-app TLS certificate expires. Green is more than 14 days, yellow is 7-14 days, and red is below 7 days.

panels.title: CDN cert days
panels.description: Days until the asset CDN TLS certificate expires. Green is more than 14 days, yellow is 7-14 days, and red is below 7 days.

panels.title: Prometheus scrape up
panels.description: Prometheus reachability for the selected Medusa target. 1 means Prometheus can scrape the exporter; 0 means the dashboard is looking at stale or missing metrics.

panels.title: Last exporter sample age
panels.description: Seconds since the Medusa exporter rendered its latest sample. Lower is fresher; a steadily rising value means scrapes or the exporter have stalled.

panels.title: Heavy snapshot age
panels.description: Age of the cached heavy metrics snapshot in seconds. Heavy database and storage rollups are current when this stays comfortably below the refresh interval.

panels.title: Exporter samples
panels.description: Number of metric samples rendered by the exporter. Sudden drops can mean collectors are missing; sudden jumps can mean cardinality changed.

panels.title: Scrape pulse
panels.description: Recent scrape availability for the selected target. A steady 100% means Prometheus has kept scraping Medusa continuously over the recent window.

panels.title: Exporter scrape and snapshot cost
panels.description: Exporter scrape duration and heavy snapshot generation duration. Higher values mean observability collection itself is taking more time.

panels.title: Collector quorum
panels.description: Share of selected exporter collectors currently reporting healthy. 100% means every selected collector succeeded on its latest run.

panels.title: In-flight jobs by type
panels.description: Queued plus running durable jobs by family. Each line is the current in-flight job count at that time, not a total over the selected dashboard range.

panels.title: Oldest active queue age
panels.description: Age of the oldest active import or Concordance queue item. Empty queues render as 0 so a missing series does not look like missing data.

targets.expr: max by (queue) (medusa_queue_oldest_age_seconds{job="$job", instance=~"$instance"}) or label_replace(max(medusa_exporter_build_info{job="$job", instance=~"$instance"}) * 0, "queue", "import", "job", ".*") or label_replace(max(medusa_exporter_build_info{job="$job", instance=~"$instance"}) * 0, "queue", "concordance", "job", ".*")

panels.title: Queue status detail
panels.description: Detailed import and Concordance job counts by status, import step, and Concordance capability. This explains which stage is filling or draining behind the top-level active-work count.

panels.title: Processing events
panels.description: Processing events grouped by kind and status. Spikes usually follow imports or Concordance runs; error-status lines identify failing stages.

panels.title: Slipstream capacity and leases
panels.description: Slipstream remote-client capacity, active leases, and runnable clients. Capacity and leases show how much remote preprocessing is available and currently occupied.

panels.title: Route latency p50 (ms)
panels.description: Median FastAPI /api/* route latency by normalized route, displayed in milliseconds. Labels drop the shared /api/ prefix; lower is faster and this line represents the typical request for each route.

panels.title: Route latency p95 (ms)
panels.description: 95th percentile FastAPI /api/* route latency by normalized route, displayed in milliseconds. Labels drop the shared /api/ prefix; this shows the slow edge of normal traffic.

panels.title: Route latency p99 (ms)
panels.description: 99th percentile FastAPI /api/* route latency by normalized route, displayed in milliseconds. Labels drop the shared /api/ prefix; this catches rare tail-latency outliers.

panels.title: Route status mix
panels.description: Count of backend routes grouped by their latest HTTP status class. 2xx should dominate; sustained 4xx/5xx counts mean routes are returning client or server errors.

targets.expr: count((medusa_backend_route_last_status{job="$job", instance=~"$instance", route=~"$route"} >= 200) and (medusa_backend_route_last_status{job="$job", instance=~"$instance", route=~"$route"} < 300)) or vector(0)

count((medusa_backend_route_last_status{job="$job", instance=~"$instance", route=~"$route"} >= 300) and (medusa_backend_route_last_status{job="$job", instance=~"$instance", route=~"$route"} < 400)) or vector(0)

count((medusa_backend_route_last_status{job="$job", instance=~"$instance", route=~"$route"} >= 400) and (medusa_backend_route_last_status{job="$job", instance=~"$instance", route=~"$route"} < 500)) or vector(0)

panels.title: Backend memory and CPU
panels.description: Backend process/container memory and CPU usage. Trends show whether API work, imports, or cache activity are increasing backend resource use.

panels.title: HAProxy traffic and errors
panels.description: HAProxy frontend sessions, byte rates, denials, retries, and errors. Traffic should move with usage; error/retry lines should generally stay near zero.

panels.title: HAProxy service health and downtime
panels.description: Per-service HAProxy downtime, time since last status change, and health-check duration. Stable services have near-zero downtime and low check duration.

panels.title: Cache hit rate
panels.description: Overall Medusa cache hit rate. Higher values mean repeated work is being served from cache instead of recomputed.

panels.title: Cache family events
panels.description: Cache hits, misses, writes, bypasses, and errors by cache family. The family split shows which app surfaces are hot, cold, bypassing, or erroring.

panels.title: Valkey memory
panels.description: Valkey used memory versus configured maximum memory. Growth toward the limit increases eviction pressure.

panels.title: Valkey keys and ops
panels.description: Valkey key count and command rate. Key growth shows cache population; operations per second show cache traffic.

panels.title: Valkey churn
panels.description: Valkey evictions, expirations, and misses. Evictions indicate memory pressure; expirations and misses show cache churn or cold paths.

panels.title: Cache and Valkey up
panels.description: Cache backend and Valkey reachability. 1 means reachable; 0 means cache-dependent paths are using fallback behavior or losing cache service.

panels.title: Library size
panels.description: Corpus size signals such as documents, pages, figures, references, and related library counts. Growth usually follows imports and Concordance enrichment.

panels.title: Citation and DOI coverage
panels.description: DOI, verified-citation, and citation-review ratios across library documents. DOI and verified-citation ratios are better when higher; needs-review ratio is better when lower.

panels.title: Extraction gaps and duplicates
panels.description: Missing extraction outputs and duplicate signals. Higher values represent library cleanup or enrichment backlog.

panels.title: Text corpus
panels.description: Parsed text, indexed words, chunks, and embedding-related corpus counts. Growth means more of the library is searchable and semantically indexed.

panels.title: Document cache
panels.description: Local document cache size versus configured limit. When used size approaches the limit, older non-active cached files become candidates for pruning.

panels.title: Local storage footprint
panels.description: Local storage bytes and file counts by area. Use it to see whether originals, processing cache, backups, model cache, or other local stores dominate disk usage.

panels.title: Database size
panels.description: Total PostgreSQL database size. Gradual growth is expected with imports; step changes usually correspond to bulk processing or maintenance.

panels.title: Relation tuple pressure
panels.description: Live and dead tuple counts by relation. Live tuples show table volume; dead tuples show cleanup pressure for vacuum.

panels.title: Vacuum and analyze age
panels.description: Time since PostgreSQL tables were vacuumed or analyzed. Lower ages mean cleanup and planner statistics are current.

panels.title: Maintenance activity and cleanup counts
panels.description: Database maintenance active time plus cleanup candidate counts. Nonzero counts show items maintenance can repair, prune, or reconcile.

panels.title: AI requests by task/status
panels.description: AI request counts by task, model, and status. The status split shows successful, failed, and in-progress AI workload volume.

panels.title: AI token totals
panels.description: AI token volume by task, model, and status. Token totals are the main driver for model usage and cost.

panels.title: AI file context and text volume
panels.description: File-context bytes and text volume sent to AI providers. Higher values mean richer document context and often higher cost or latency.

panels.title: Composition spend (USD)
panels.description: Document-composition spend by stage and status. Provider/model labels are collapsed here so local deterministic work and external API calls do not create noisy model-looking legends.

panels.title: Composition speed
panels.description: Document-composition duration by stage and status. Lower lines are faster; local in the underlying metric means deterministic/on-box processing, not a local GPT model.

panels.title: Latest AI failure age
panels.description: Seconds since the latest failed AI usage ledger call. Lower values mean a recent failure; rising values mean no newer AI failure has been recorded.

panels.title: Model pricing freshness
panels.description: Age of current model-pricing records by provider and price basis. Lower values mean fresher pricing metadata; rising values mean estimates may need a pricing refresh.

panels.title: Latest backup age
panels.description: Age of the latest known backup. Lower values mean backups are recent; rising age means the latest verified recovery point is getting older.

panels.title: Latest backup size
panels.description: Size of the latest backup artifact. Size changes reflect corpus/database growth or a change in backup scope.

panels.title: Latest backup verified
panels.description: Verification state for the latest backup. 1 means backup verification passed; 0 means unverified, failed, or unavailable.

panels.title: Maintenance window ready
panels.description: Release maintenance readiness. 1 means Medusa reports an idle maintenance window; 0 means active sessions or work are present.

panels.title: Backup run state
panels.description: Backup runs by phase and status over time. The timeline shows whether backups are initializing, dumping, uploading, verifying, complete, or failed.

panels.title: Release flags
panels.description: Release, reload, apply, and dirty-checkout flags over time. Nonzero lines indicate an available update, reload recommendation, active apply state, or dirty checkout.

panels.title: Maintenance sessions
panels.description: Active maintenance sessions and blockers. Zero means no active maintenance/session blockers are reported; nonzero means maintenance should wait.
